paragraphs: The current UPI rewards ecosystem is fragmented, with users earning non-transferable rewards across different apps such as Google Pay, PhonePe, and Paytm, resulting in inconsistent user experiences and limited utility of rewards. This fragmentation presents a strategic opportunity to introduce a standardized rewards system that enhances user satisfaction by making rewards portable and increasing their perceived value. In response, the issuer has decided to implement a unified rewards system, enabling users to track and redeem rewards seamlessly across multiple UPI apps through a single dashboard, thus aligning with government initiatives for digital financial inclusion and setting a new market standard.

The National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) has decided to implement a unified rewards system for UPI transactions, aimed at standardizing and enhancing the user experience across different UPI applications.

This decision impacts the UPI App modules, specifically the reward management components within Google Pay, PhonePe, Paytm, and Amazon Pay, which will be integrated into a centralized rewards dashboard.
The technical artifacts affected include the UPI rewards API, which will be extended to support cross-app reward aggregation and redemption functionalities.
Modifications will be required in the UPI transaction schemas to accommodate reward data interoperability and ensure seamless integration with the new unified rewards dashboard.

Payment Service Provider (PSP) Banks must integrate the unified rewards system into their existing UPI applications. This integration requires the adoption of standardized APIs provided by NPCI to ensure seamless rewards tracking and redemption across different UPI platforms.

Issuer/Remitter Banks must update their transaction processing systems to support the new rewards data structure. This includes ensuring that all transaction data is accurately reported to the unified rewards dashboard, facilitating real-time rewards accumulation and redemption.

Beneficiary/Payee Banks are advised to collaborate with NPCI to ensure that reward credits are processed efficiently. This involves aligning their systems with the standardized rewards framework to enable smooth interoperability across different banking platforms.

UPI App developers must incorporate the unified rewards dashboard within their user interfaces. This requires adherence to NPCI's design guidelines to provide a consistent and user-friendly experience for tracking and redeeming rewards.

NPCI must ensure the availability and reliability of the centralized rewards management infrastructure. This includes maintaining the security and integrity of the rewards data and providing ongoing support and updates to all ecosystem participants.
Please disseminate the information contained herein to the officials concerned.

The unified rewards system for UPI transactions is designed to streamline the earning and redemption of rewards across different UPI applications. This feature will centralize rewards, allowing users to manage them from a single dashboard, thereby enhancing user satisfaction and engagement.

Product managers must ensure that the integration of the unified rewards system aligns with the existing UPI app functionalities. They are advised to coordinate with engineering teams to facilitate seamless implementation and user experience.

Engineering teams must prioritize the development of the unified rewards dashboard, ensuring compatibility with all participating UPI apps. They must also address any technical challenges related to interoperability and data synchronization.

Compliance teams must review the unified rewards system to ensure adherence to regulatory standards and guidelines. They are advised to monitor the implementation process and provide necessary compliance support to product and engineering teams.
